description: Mandatory elements that must be present in all SoW documents

requiredSections.description: High-level overview of project goals, scope, and business value
requiredSections.errorMessage: Executive Summary is required and must be at least 100 characters

requiredSections.description: Clear definition of what is in-scope and out-of-scope
requiredSections.errorMessage: At least one scope item is required with clear in/out designation
At least one deliverable is required with acceptance criteria

requiredSections.description: How the project will be executed using selected methodology
requiredSections.errorMessage: Delivery approach must be at least 200 characters and align with methodology

requiredSections.description: Explicit list of what customer must provide/do
requiredSections.errorMessage: Customer responsibilities must be explicitly documented

requiredSections.description: Operations and support plan for transitioning solution to customer (SOW Drafting Guidance - Fundamentals)
requiredSections.errorMessage: Support transition plan required or mark as N/A with justification
recommendedElements: Hypercare period duration and coverage (e.g., 30 days with 8x5 coverage)
Training plan for customer IT team
examples: Post-migration, Microsoft will provide 30 days of hypercare support with 8x5 coverage. This includes monitoring, issue resolution, and performance optimization. Knowledge transfer sessions will be conducted during weeks 11-12 covering Azure operations, monitoring, and incident response procedures. After hypercare, customer IT team will assume full operational responsibility with option to purchase extended support.

Customer will assume full operational support upon go-live. Microsoft will provide 2 weeks of knowledge transfer including documentation, runbook walkthroughs, and shadowing support tickets. Customer confirms existing incident management process and 24x7 support team capacity.

requiredSections.description: Project assumptions that must hold true
requiredSections.errorMessage: At least one assumption must be documented
At least one risk must be identified with mitigation strategy

acceptanceCriteria.errorMessage: Each deliverable MUST have specific, measurable acceptance criteria (SOW Writing Guide v2.1)
guidelines: Include success metrics or test criteria
Include evidence of completion (e.g., approval, test results, documentation)
goodExamples: All 50 VMs successfully migrated and operational in Azure with zero S1 defects; validation testing completed with sign-off from customer infrastructure team
Architecture documentation including network diagrams, resource groups, and security policies reviewed and approved by customer CTO
Runbook includes pre-migration checklist, migration steps, validation procedures, and rollback plan; approved by migration team lead
All security controls documented and validated against customer security baseline; compliance scan results with zero critical findings
User acceptance testing completed with 95% pass rate and all S1/S2 defects resolved
All unit tests passing with 80%+ code coverage and approved by technical lead

customerResponsibilitiesGuidelines.description: Customer dependencies and obligations must be explicitly documented in SOW (SOW Drafting Guidance)
suggestedItems: Provide timely access to all systems, data, documentation, and credentials
Assign dedicated Product Owner/decision-maker with authority
Assign dedicated infrastructure SME available for daily coordination
Participate in scheduled meetings and reviews
Provide test data and validation resources
Make decisions within agreed timeframes (specify: e.g., 2 business days)
Execute user acceptance testing within specified timeframe
Provide production environment access within X business days of request
Approve migration runbooks and provide sign-off for each wave
agileSpecificRequirements: Empower Product Owner with decision-making authority and budget control
Assign Scrum Master (can be Microsoft or customer)
Assign Application/Business Owners for each workstream
Participate in Sprint Planning, Daily Standups, Sprint Reviews, and Retrospectives
Prioritize backlog items before each sprint (within X business days)
Provide timely approval/feedback within sprint cycle
Maintain Definition of Ready for backlog items
Validate deliverables against Definition of Done
Provide availability for agile ceremonies (specify frequency and duration)
customerResponsibilitiesGuidelines.errorMessage: Customer must have clear, explicit responsibilities documented with specific obligations
validationRules: Each responsibility should be specific and actionable
Include timeframes where applicable (e.g., 'within 2 business days')
Avoid vague terms like 'as needed' or 'timely' without definition
Consider adding consequences for unmet dependencies (e.g., Change Request process)

mitigation.errorMessage: Each risk must have a documented mitigation plan

owner.description: Recommended to assign risk owner for high/critical risks
